content.full_text:
a\
I
BRCCHURTS.
\YOUT. PROMOTION, MARI
ATIVITY. COPY VVRITING
April20,1990
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 " J' Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Gentlemen:
After receiving a telephone call from Maureen Swanson of your office regarding a brochure one of
my clients had me create, and at her suggestion, I am asking your help in clarifying what should
and shoulC not be included in publieations such as brochures, new-sletters and the like as far as
names, photographs, etc. of elected school board of education members, in fact, any type of elected
public offrcial, but particularly those involved in school business.
As an entrepreneur who began business about seven years ago, I have specialized in creating
brochures, fliers, newsletters and the like at my clients direction. I am most anxious that my clients
be aware of any and all regulations that affect the publications I do for them. I am very proud of the
quality work I do and the service I provide, and it is my intention for the publications I create to be
correct and in compliance with all regulations. (I deliver the entire job to the client or the clients
agent, and each client is responsible for his or her own distribution, mailed or otherwise.)
In creating publications, I am often asked to include the names, and in some cases phone numbers
and./or photographs of elected board members. As a consultant, everything I do (photographs, stories,
mastheads, etc.) is at the direction of my clients, but I want to be informed so that I can advise them
what is correct and their publications can be in compliance with all regulations.
Enclosed is a copy of a 1987 newsletter similar to the type I currently create for numerous clients.
This publication does have a mailer and my clients intended to mail it to families of students. I
would like to have your opinion on this type of publication for future editions.
Also, I create numerous brochures that are not intended for mailing, but do include names (and in
some instances photographs) of elected officials. It is my understanding that these publications are
often provided to chambers of commerce, Realtors, and the like, and more than 200 of them could
conceivably be sent out in a bulk mailing. Please provide me with an opinion on the correctness of
what should be included in these brochures.
For further information, please feel free to contact me.
Enclosure
cc: Maureen Swanson , FPPC
b$ryrha*aa--
701 H|GH STREET. SU|TE202. AUBURN, CA 95603. (916) 885-6097

sections.question: After receiving a telephone call from Maureen Swanson of your office regarding a brochure one of my clients had me create, and at her suggestion, I am asking your help in clarifying what should and should not be included in publications such as brochures, newsletters and the like as far as names, photographs, etc. of elected school board of education members, in fact, any type of elected public official, but particularly those involved in school business. In creating publications, I am often asked to include the names, and in some cases phone numbers and/or photographs of elected board members. I would like to have your opinion on this type of publication for future editions. Please provide me with an opinion on the correctness of what should be included in these brochures.
sections.question_synthetic: A consultant who creates brochures and newsletters seeks guidance on what information (names, photographs, phone numbers) about elected school board members and other public officials may be legally included in publications that may be distributed to 200+ recipients.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present. Document contains a request for advice on compliance with regulations regarding inclusion of elected officials' information in publications. Letter references an enclosed 1987 newsletter sample.

In creating publications, I am often asked to include the names, and in some cases phone numbers and/or photographs of elected board members. I would like to have your opinion on this type of publication for future editions. Please provide me with an opinion on the correctness of what should be included in these brochures.
